title: Graph Database (Amazon Neptune)
description: Amazon Neptune is a fully-managed graph database supporting both property graphs (Gremlin) and RDF graphs (SPARQL).

provides.reasoning: Amazon Neptune is fundamentally a fully-managed graph database service, providing graph database functionality as its core offering.

provides.description: AWS managed graph storage with Gremlin and SPARQL support
provides.reasoning: Amazon Neptune provides the underlying storage mechanism for graph data structures with vertices and edges, managing both property graphs and RDF graphs.
Amazon Neptune natively supports Apache TinkerPop Gremlin for property graph queries, this is a key capability consumers would depend on.
Amazon Neptune supports SPARQL for RDF graph queries, enabling semantic web applications - this is a distinct query capability.
Amazon Neptune automatically replicates data across multiple Availability Zones within a region, providing built-in high availability with automatic failover to read replicas.

provides.description: Up to 15 read replicas for query scaling
provides.reasoning: Amazon Neptune supports up to 15 read replicas for scaling read operations, enabling horizontal read scalability.
As a fully-managed service, Neptune handles provisioning, patching, backup, and recovery, eliminating operational overhead.

requires.reasoning: Amazon Neptune runs within a VPC and requires VPC configuration for network isolation and security.
Amazon Neptune integrates with AWS IAM for database authentication and access control.

query_language.description: Query language support: Gremlin for property graphs (labeled nodes/edges), SPARQL for RDF graphs (semantic triples), or both for dual-model workloads

instance_type.description: Amazon Neptune instance type: t3 for dev/test (burstable), r5 for production (memory-optimized). Larger instances support more concurrent queries and graph traversals

read_replicas.description: Number of read replica instances for scaling read throughput and improving availability across AZs

backup_retention_days.description: Automatic backup retention period in days. Longer retention increases storage costs but provides more recovery options

high_availability.description: Enable multi-AZ deployment with automatic failover. Recommended for production workloads requiring 99.99% availability

encryption_at_rest.description: Enable encryption at rest using AWS KMS. Required for compliance with security standards (HIPAA, PCI-DSS)

service_auth_method.description: Amazon Neptune uses IAM database authentication. 'iam_role' (EC2/Lambda execution role) is preferred over static IAM user credentials.

ssl_enforced.description: Neptune enforces TLS/SSL on all connections. Cannot be disabled.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Amazon Neptune pricing: Min represents smallest production setup with db.t3.medium instance ($0.348/hr × 730hrs = $254) + 100GB storage ($0.10/GB = $10) + I/O costs ($0.20/million requests ≈ $76) totaling ~$340/month. Max represents production cluster with db.r5.2xlarge primary ($2.90/hr = $2,117) + 2 read replicas ($2.90/hr × 2 = $4,234) + 1TB storage ($100) + backup storage 500GB ($50) + higher I/O costs ($1,999) totaling ~$8,500/month. Neptune has no free tier and requires 24/7 runtime. Dev environments typically $340-700/month, production $2,000-8,500/month depending on scale.
provenance.adoptionCost: High complexity adoption: Graph database expertise is specialized - team needs to learn graph modeling concepts (vertices, edges, properties), Gremlin/SPARQL query languages (steep learning curve, 40-60 hours training), data migration from relational to graph structures (complex transformation, 60-100 hours), AWS Neptune-specific features (IAM integration, VPC setup, backup strategies, 20-30 hours). Schema design for graphs differs fundamentally from relational databases. Implementation includes: proof-of-concept (80 hours), production setup with high availability (40 hours), query optimization (40 hours), monitoring setup (20 hours). Total ~240-280 hours at $100-150/hour blended rate = $3,000-4,000. Graph databases represent paradigm shift requiring significant learning investment.
provenance.licenseCost: No separate license cost - Neptune is a fully-managed AWS service with pay-as-you-go pricing model. All costs are usage-based (compute, storage, I/O) included in monthly operational expenses. No upfront licenses, enterprise agreements, or per-user fees required. AWS account is the only prerequisite.

supports_nfr.reason: Amazon Neptune managed service with Multi-AZ deployment supports up to 99.99% availability.
Graph database queries have inherent traversal complexity requiring at least 50ms p95 latency.
Complex graph traversals and network overhead establish a minimum 200ms p99 latency floor.
Amazon Neptune automated Multi-AZ failover typically completes in 3-5 minutes for write instance recovery.
Amazon Neptune instance capacity limits peak throughput to approximately 10,000 queries per second or 36M per hour.
Amazon Neptune graph database can handle up to approximately 5,000 read queries per second with read replicas.
Amazon Neptune supports up to approximately 10,000 write queries per second on the primary instance.
Amazon Neptune integrates with CloudWatch Logs and CloudTrail for audit logging when enabled.
Amazon Neptune supports all tenant isolation strategies from row-level security to separate database instances.
Amazon Neptune supports PII storage with encryption at rest via KMS and encryption in transit via TLS.
Amazon Neptune supports GDPR compliance with encryption, data retention controls, and right to deletion capabilities.
Amazon Neptune is HIPAA eligible with proper configuration including encryption at rest and access controls.
Amazon Neptune supports CCPA compliance requirements through encryption, access controls, and data deletion features.
Amazon Neptune supports SOX compliance through encryption, audit logging, and access control mechanisms.
Amazon Neptune supports read-your-writes consistency when reading from the writer instance or eventually consistent reads from replicas.
Amazon Neptune provides strict durability through synchronous Multi-AZ replication and continuous S3 backups.

supports_constraints.reason: Amazon Neptune is an AWS-specific managed graph database service.
Amazon Neptune is accessible via Gremlin and SPARQL APIs from any programming language with appropriate client libraries.
Amazon Neptune is a backend database service accessible from any platform type via network connections.
Amazon Neptune can be used with or without an external caching layer depending on application read patterns.
Amazon Neptune can be integrated with async messaging systems or used synchronously depending on architecture.
Amazon Neptune can store graph data for AI/ML applications or be used for non-AI workloads.
Amazon Neptune supports both single-tenant and multi-tenant application architectures with various isolation strategies.
Amazon Neptune can handle both real-time queries and batch graph processing workloads.
Amazon Neptune supports ACID transactions within a single graph and can participate in distributed patterns.
Amazon Neptune supports change streams for real-time data synchronization and can be used without streaming.
Amazon Neptune can be used alongside vector search systems or independently for graph-only workloads.
Amazon Neptune is a graph database but can coexist with document stores in hybrid architectures.
Amazon Neptune focuses on graph relationships but can be used alongside key-value stores in polyglot persistence.
Amazon Neptune is specifically a graph database requiring the graph_database feature flag to be enabled.
Amazon Neptune can model time-series data in graphs or be used for non-temporal graph data.
Amazon Neptune supports transactional graph queries (OLTP) and analytical graph workloads.
Amazon Neptune supports both analytical graph queries (OLAP) and transactional workloads depending on use case.

warn_nfr.reason: Graph databases have operational overhead (Multi-AZ, managed infrastructure); at very low QPS, simpler relational or NoSQL solutions may be more cost-effective.
warn_nfr.message: db-graph--neptune: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (<1). Amazon Neptune's infrastructure overhead may not be justified at this scale; consider simpler storage options.
